Latest News

Spurs Break Thunder's 16-Game Win Streak En Route to NBA Cup Final

By Rohan Jain · Dec 19, 2025
Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs secured a 111-109 win on Saturday • Dec 14, 2025 • Getty Images

The Thunder’s pristine 24-1 tag, tying the single-season record through 25 games, has dented slightly after the Spurs narrowly etched a win in the NBA Cup semifinals.

Oklahoma City took an early 11-point lead through the first quarter in the semi-final matchup between two of the brightest young teams. To start the game, the Spurs missed 12 three-pointers and could only muster 20 points to the Thunder’s 31.

Victor Wembanyama’s entrance in the second quarter sparked a 13-0 run to bring the Spurs back into the contest. Despite trailing by as much as 16 early in the second, the game was within a single possession, 49-46 in favor of OKC, at halftime.

Through the third and fourth quarters, neither team had control of the game, and as the Spurs took their first lead of the game, 10 more lead changes were to come Both teams nailed clutch shots in the final quarter, but the Spurs were ahead two with just 1.5 seconds remaining, Jalen Williams having one shot remaining from the line. Alex Caruso caught the intentionally missed free throw but was unable to finish the putback in a controversial no-call.

Devin Vassell finished with 23 points, along with 22 from each of Stephon Castle, De’Aaron Fox, and Victor Wembanyama in their win. The top scorer for the Thunder was the 2025 MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, accompanied by four more players in double-digits. Wembanyama ended with a +21 plus/minus in 21 minutes.

The Spurs advance to face the New York Knicks in the 2025 NBA Cup and have notably curbed the Thunder’s aspiration of holding the best record in league history.

About The Merger

Born from the merger of The Centurion Mail and Streamline, we are a next-generation media organization launching June 2026.

Our Mission

At The Merger, we offer daily social media content, online articles, and video production. It is not just about content - it is about building a sustainable workforce that actively contributes to a credible journalistic portfolio. We cover U.S. news, global news, sports, technology, medicine, and more with the highest journalistic standards.

Active Divisions

⚙

Management

Operations, HR, Finance, and Division Leads. 8 people across 4 teams.

📱

Social Media

Coverage, Caption, and Design teams on TikTok and Instagram. 7+ contributors.

📰

Online Articles

Division Lead, EICs, Editors, and Staffers producing quality journalism. 9+ contributors.

🎬

Video Production

Story Producers, Editors, and Researchers creating video content for YouTube. 6+ contributors.

Join The Merger

We are looking for passionate writers, editors, designers, videographers, and social media creators to join our team.

Open Positions

The Merger has positions across all four of our active divisions. Whether you are interested in writing articles, producing videos, creating social media content, or helping manage operations, there is a place for you.

Staffers - Online Articles

Research and produce high-quality articles following journalistic standards.

Editors - Online Articles

Write articles and mentor staffers to improve their writing skills.

Coverage Team - Social Media

Select trending content and cover breaking news efficiently.

Caption Team - Social Media

Write captions for daily social media content following journalism standards.

Design Team - Social Media

Create new templates for daily social media posts following design trends.

Editors & Researchers - Video Production

Produce video content and research trending topics.

Partner with The Merger to reach an engaged audience passionate about news, sports, entertainment, and culture.

🤝

Media Partners

📢

Sponsors
